People/Miller-Mathias-Tallman.title: Miller Mathias Tallman
People/Miller-Mathias-Tallman.content:
Miller Mathias Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Miller Mathias Tallman
Role in this project: Tallman-line ancestor connected to Ault-line census households.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 14 Apr 1841; died 8 Apr 1921 (age 79 years, 11 months, 25 days).
Birthplace: Ohio
Occupations: Farmer (1880), teamster (1900)
Military Service: Iowa Soldiers’ Home resident (1910-1920), suggesting Union veteran status
Burial: Iowa Veterans’ Home Cemetery, Marshalltown, Iowa; South Section, Row 12, Grave 4
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller page image for page 241 identifies Miller Mathias Tallman as a child of Romancy Miller and Benjamin Tallman and gives his birth date as 14 Apr 1841.

Census Records and Life Progression
1880 Iowa Census — Cherokee County, Afton Township (as farmer)

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, married, age 39, occupation farmer, born Ohio
Wife: Elizabeth TALLMAN (née Ault), married, age 30, born Ohio, occupation keeping house
Children:

Willis G. TALLMAN, male, single, age 6, born Iowa
Lena TALLMAN, female, single, age 1, born Iowa


Household also includes:

Elbert WINN, male, single, age 17, born Iowa
Mary AULT (Elizabeth’s mother), female, widow, age 73, born Maine, occupation aged
Andrew RICHEY, male, single, age 24, born Illinois, occupation farm laborer


Source: Fam Hist Lib Film 1254332, Page 52D; GSU microfilm available

1900 Iowa Census — Woodbury County, Sioux City, p. 231R, Filmore Avenue (as teamster)

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, birthdate Apr 1841, age 58, occupation teamster
Wife: Elizabeth P. TALLMAN (née Ault), race White, birthdate Oct 1841, age 58
Child:

Lena P. TALLMAN, female, race White, birthdate Feb 1879, age 21


Note: Household in Sioux City; occupation changed from farmer to teamster (urban/transport labor)
Source: Series T623, Roll 467, Page 231B; GSU microfilm available

1910 Iowa Census — Marshall County, Linn Township, Iowa Soldiers’ Home

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, age 69, occupation none
Wife: Elizabeth P. TALLMAN, female, race White, age 68
Note: Residing at Iowa Soldiers’ Home; both listed as members
Source: Series T624, Roll 441, Page 72; GSU microfilm available

1920 Iowa Census — Marshall County, Iowa Soldiers’ Home

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, age 78, occupation none, member of Soldiers’ Home
Wife: Elizabeth TALLMAN, female, race White, age 78, member of Soldiers’ Home
Note: Continued residence at Iowa Soldiers’ Home; married couple both identified as members
Source: Series T625, Roll 502, Pages 5B, ED 155; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Wife: Elizabeth Plomey Ault (1841-1926), married c. 1880
Children identified: Willis G. Tallman (b. ~1874), Lena/Lena P. Tallman (b. 1879)
Mother-in-law: Mary Ault (b. ~1802), living with household in 1880
Pedigree connection: Married into Frederick Ault’s family; likely Union veteran based on Iowa Soldiers’ Home residence
Compiled-book reconciliation issue: The Miller genealogy page 242 says Mathias M. Tallman married Lizzie Wimmer on 24 Aug 1872 and lists children William Gilmore Tallman and Elvin Lenora Tallman. The same page also summarizes the children as Willis G. Tallman and Lenore Tallman; page 250 identifies Elvin Lenora Tallman as wife of U. B. Thorpe. These details likely correspond to the census-linked Elizabeth/Willis/Lena family group, but marriage and vital records are still needed before treating Lizzie Wimmer as resolved to Elizabeth Plomey Ault.

Family Diagram

graph TD
  MMT["Miller Mathias Tallman<br/>1841-1921<br/>Farmer to Teamster to Veteran"]
  EPA["Elizabeth Plomey Ault<br/>1841-1926"]
  
  WG["Willis G. Tallman<br/>b. ~1874"]
  LT["Lenore Hetty Tallman<br/>Elvin Lenora?<br/>1879-1953"]
  
  MMT --- EPA
  MMT --> WG
  MMT --> LT

Miller Mathias demonstrates the trajectory of a Civil War veteran: farming in rural Iowa (1880), transitioning to urban teamster work in Sioux City (1900), and finally entering the Iowa Soldiers’ Home for long-term care (1910-1920) until his death in 1921.
Research Gaps

Confirm Union military service record and identify regiment/unit.
Locate Willis G. Tallman in later records (1880 shows age 6, implying birth ~1874).
Trace Lena P. Tallman after 1900; identify marriage and descendants.
Clarify relationship of Elbert Winn and Andrew Richey to the household in 1880.
Determine exact reason for Iowa Soldiers’ Home admission (age, health, pension status).
Resolve the wife-name conflict between Elizabeth Plomey Ault in census-linked sources and Lizzie Wimmer in the Miller genealogy.
Determine whether William Gilmore Tallman and Willis G. Tallman are the same person.
Determine whether Elvin Lenora Tallman and Lenore Hetty Tallman are the same person.

People/Nathan-Spicer.title: Nathan Spicer
People/Nathan-Spicer.content:
Nathan Spicer
Biographical Profile

Name: Nathan Spicer
Birth/Death: Born 3 Apr 1796; died 16 May 1873 (age 77 years, 1 month, 13 days)
Role in this project: Spicer line patriarch; grandfather of George B. Spicer; patriarch of Iowa Spicer agricultural line (1850s-1870s).
Burial: Spicer/Spooner Cemetery, Homestead, Iowa; Grave 13 on Highway 6, 1.5 miles east; inscription NATHAN SPICER / DIED / May 16, 1873 / AGED / 77yrs. 1mo. 13ds; Burial Sites book, page 34.

Census Records and Household Context
1850 Iowa Census — Johnson County, Iowa City, District 9

Head: Henry Usher, male, age 37, blacksmith, born New York
Wife: Maria Usher, female, age 36, born New York (Nathan’s daughter)
Children: Henry Usher Jr., age 9; Martha Farbanks, age 6 (both Iowa)
Related: Nathaniel Spicer, male, age 54, blacksmith, born New York (Nathan — patriarch)
Other family: Henry Spicer, male, age 20, Ohio; Zura Spicer, female, age 18, Ohio (likely Nathan’s children)
Note: Nathan appears in daughter Maria’s household as aging patriarch
Source: Series M432, Roll 185, Page 148

1860 Iowa Census — Iowa County, Iowa Township

Head: Wm Spicer, male, age 42, farmer, born New York (Nathan’s son William)
Wife: Deliha Spicer, female, age 43, born Ohio
Children: Mary C. Spicer, female, age 6, Iowa
Nathan Spicer, male, age 64, farm laborer, born New York (aging patriarch, now dependent)
Other: Wm Ferguson, male, age 55, farm laborer, born Ohio
Source: Series M653, Roll 325, Page 628

1870 Iowa Census — Iowa County, Amana Township

Head: William Spicer, male, age 54, farmer, born New York
Wife: Delilah Spicer, female, age 58, keeping house, born Ohio
Nathan Spicer, male, age 74, listed as “without prospect” (no occupation), born New York
Note: Nathan now elderly (74) in son William’s household; no occupation listed
Property: William’s farm valued at 1500; personal property 600
Source: Series M593, Roll 396, Page 259

Family Connections


Children identified:

Maria Usher (daughter, married Henry Usher, blacksmith)
Henry Spicer (son, age 20 in 1850, Ohio-born)
Zura Spicer (daughter, age 18 in 1850, Ohio-born)
William Spicer (son, major farmer with 1500 acres in 1860-1870)
Charles Russell Spicer (son? — lineage suggests Nathan → Charles Russell → George B.)



Son William Spicer: Farmer, estate valued $1500, supports aging father in 1860s


Grandchildren: Through William and Delilah: Mary C. Spicer visible in 1860


Household trajectory:

1850: Aging patriarch (54) visiting daughter Maria’s blacksmith household
1860: Dependent in son William’s farming household (age 64, farm laborer)
1870: Fully dependent elderly (age 74, “without prospect”) in son’s household



Burial and Legacy

Burial: Spicer/Spooner Cemetery, Homestead, Iowa (same cemetery where George B. Spicer’s wife Hattie May Risden was eventually buried)
Legacy: Patriarch of Spicer line spanning 77 years (1796-1873); established in Iowa by 1850; sons continued agricultural prosperity (William with 1500-acre farm by 1860)

Three-Generation Spicer Progression

Nathan Spicer (1796-1873): Patriarch, emigrated from New York/Ohio; established in Iowa by 1850
Charles Russell Spicer (1822-1887): Son; farmer, chair maker, bridge generation
George B. Spicer (1864-1938): Grandson; major patriarch with large household, 7+ children; prosperous farmer

Research Gaps

Confirm spouse name(s) for Nathan — appears unnamed in census summaries
Clarify exact lineage: Nathan → Charles Russell → George B. (son→son or other relationship?)
Verify death date and location from vital records (Homestead burial suggests Iowa County residence)
Explore Henry and Zura Spicer (children, Ohio-born, date/context unclear)

Rebecca Blake
Biographical Profile

Name: Rebecca Blake
Role in this project: Lemmon-linked ancestor represented in 1850 Ohio census-summary household extract.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 26 Oct 1779; died 29 Mar 1855 (age 75 years, 5 months, 3 days per burial record).
Burial: Tew Cemetery, Townsend Township, Sandusky County, Ohio; Section 25-17-5, Coordinates 412203N 0825125W; inscription reads REBECCA / wife of / JAMES LEMMON / DIED / Mar. 29, 1855 / Aged / 75 Yrs. 5.M. 3d; Burial Sites book, page 9.
Spouse: James Lemmon (1779-1854); married name: Lemmon
The Sandusky-Beers sketch for John McIntyre Lemmon states that James Lemmon Sr. married Rebecca Blake in 1805 and identifies them as John McIntyre Lemmon’s grandparents.

Census Records and Household Context
1850 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township

Head: James Lemmon, male, age 71, farmer, born Pennsylvania
Rebecca Lemmon (spouse), female, age 71, born Pennsylvania
Household composition:

Mathew? Lemmon, male, age 38, farmer, born New York (son)
Sarah Lemmon, female, age 21, born New York (daughter) — later Sarah Annett Lemmon
Frank Lemmon, male, age 1, born Ohio (grandson or younger son)
Nathan Harkins, male, age 12, born New York (boarder/relative)
David Lemmon, male, age 21, farmer, born New York (son or relative)


Source: Series M432, Roll 726, Page 476, R/F 1173/1198; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Husband: James Lemmon (b. 1779 Pennsylvania) — farmer, patriarch of Ohio Lemmon line
Children identified: Uriah Blake Lemmon from the Sandusky-Beers sketch, plus Matthew, Sarah (later Annett), Frank, and David from census extraction where exact relationships still need image-level confirmation.
Grandson: John McIntyre Lemmon
Age at 1850 census: 71 years old (born 1779)
Pedigree significance: Links the Blake surname to Lemmon family; appears on same timeline chart as Uriah Blake Lemmon and Uriah Blake Thorpe
Burial location: Same cemetery (Tew) as husband James Lemmon, adjacent plots in same section

People/Romancy-Miller.title: Romancy Miller
People/Romancy-Miller.content:
Romancy Miller
Biographical Profile

Name: Romancy Miller (later Romancy Tallman)
Role in this project: Tallman-line matriarch spanning Iowa (1850-1910) with six-decade household documentation.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 10 Apr 1819; died 31 Jan 1911 (age 91 years, 8 months, 21 days).
Maiden surname: Miller; married name: Tallman (married Benjamin Tallman c. 1840s)
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller page image for page 240 identifies Romancy Miller as a child of Mathias Miller and Amy E. Critton.
Page 240-241 says Romancy Miller married Benjamin F. Tallman on 25 Oct 1834 at Millersport, Ohio; moved to Canal Winchester, Ohio, about 1845; moved to Jones County, Iowa, in 1848; later lived in Rippey, Greene County, Iowa; and was affiliated with the M.E. Church.

Census Records and Life Progression
1850 Iowa Census — Jones County, Rome Township

William L TALMON, male, age 2, born Iowa

Head: Benj TALLMAN, male, age 59, occupation farmer, born Ohio
Romancee TALLMAN (wife), female, age 56, born Ohio
Children:

Romancy TALLMAN (mother), female, race White, age 91, widowed, occupation none

Source: Series T624, Roll 403, Page 179; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Husband: Benjamin Tallman; the Miller genealogy names him as Benjamin F. Tallman, while the vault page currently uses Benjamin B. Tallman from existing records.
Children identified: Miller, Emma E., Sarah J., Eliza E., Nathaniel H., Ama E., Griffin C., William L., Jacob M., Romancy (10+ children across 60-year span)
Son: Miller Mathias Tallman (possible relationship to household members)
Daughters: Amy E. Garnant, Eliza Lieberknecht (living with them in later years)

People/Rosanna-Campbell.title: Rosanna Campbell
People/Rosanna-Campbell.content:
Rosanna Campbell
Biographical Profile

Name: Rosanna Campbell
Role in this project: McIntyre-line spouse named in the MacIntyre book outprint and pedigree timeline extraction.

Source-Cited Facts

The MacIntyre book outprint says Rosanna Campbell was born 14 January 1756.
The same source says she married William McIntyre at Chester, Massachusetts, on 25 July 1778.
The source lists William and Rosanna Campbell McIntyre’s children as Betsey, Thomas, William, Zimri, Anna, Major, and Ananias.

Samuel Miller
Biographical Profile

Name: Samuel Miller
Role in this project: Older compiled Miller-line ancestor in the Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller outprint.

Source-Cited Facts

The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller outprint says Samuel Miller was probably born in York County, Pennsylvania, about 1738, while noting that no definite birth record had been found.
The same source says Samuel Miller married Magdalena Wiley about 1758 or 1759, but presents this as a deduction rather than a record-backed fact.
The source places Samuel Miller in York County, Pennsylvania, tax records in 1777.
The source says Samuel Miller purchased land in Rockingham County, Virginia, in 1782, about two miles west of Harrisonburg.
The source says Samuel Miller probably died in 1789.
The source lists known children as David, Elizabeth, Christly, Michael, Abraham, Barbara, John, Anna, Samuel, and Magdalena.

Family Connections

Spouse: Magdalena Wiley, based on the compiled Miller genealogy.
Child: John Miller.

Research Gaps

Verify Samuel Miller’s York County tax entry and Rockingham County land purchase in primary records.
Confirm the marriage to Magdalena Wiley from church, land, probate, or family Bible evidence.

Sarah Annett Lemmon
Biographical Profile

Name: Sarah Annett Lemmon
Role in this project: Lemmon-to-Thorp/Thorpe branch ancestor represented across 1850-1880 census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 21 Mar 1841; died 6 Aug 1886 (age 45 years, 7 months, 7 days per burial record).
Burial: Oakland Cemetery, Beaman, Iowa; Lot 21; inscription reads SARAH ANNETT THORP / DIED AUG. 6, 1886 / AGED 45 YRS. 7M. 7D.; Burial Sites book, page 15.
Maiden surname: Lemmon; married name: Thorp/Thorpe (Monroe/Munroe Thorpe)

Census Records and Life Progression

Head: Euriah B. Lemmon, male, farmer
Sarah A. Lemmon appears in Uriah Blake Lemmon’s household as a daughter
Age: ~9 years old
Source: Series M432, Roll 726, Pages 476-477; GSU microfilm available

1860 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township (as wife)

Head: Munroe Thorp, male (spelling variant of Monroe)
Sarah Thorp, female, married
Children: Emily J. Thorp
Other household member: Omar Tanner, farm laborer
Source: Series M653, Roll 1032, Page 54; GSU microfilm available

Head: Monore Thorp, male, age ~40, retired farmer, born New York
Sarah Thorp, female, age ~29, keeping house, born Ohio
Children:

Hattie Thorp, female, at home
Nettie Thorp, female, at home
Clyde Thorp, male, age ~5 (born September in Iowa)


Other household member: Omar Tanner, farm laborer
Source: Series M593, Roll 410, Page 486; GSU microfilm available

1880 Iowa Census — Grundy County, Clay Township

Head: M. Thorpe (Monroe), male, age ~52, farmer, born New York
Sarah Thorpe, female, age ~39, keeping house, born Ohio
Children in household:

Hattie Thorpe, daughter, age ~23, at home
Nettie Thorpe, daughter, age ~21, school teacher
Clyde Thorpe, son, age ~17, at home
Gertrude Thorpe, daughter, age ~15, at home
U.B. Thorpe, son, age ~13, at home


Boarder: Ch. Richards, male, laborer
Source: Series T9, Roll 341, Page 388C, Fam Hist Lib Film 1254341

Family Connections

Father: Uriah Blake Lemmon (1808-1887) — Ohio patriarch
Husband: Monroe (Munroe) Thorpe (b. ~1828 New York) — farmer in Ohio and Iowa
Children identified: Emily J., Hattie, Nettie, Clyde, Gertrude, U.B. (Uriah Blake?)
Lemmon branch: Direct child of Uriah Blake Lemmon; carries Lemmon name forward as “Annett” to Thorpe line
Pedigree connection: Links the Lemmon and Thorpe family branches through marriage

People/Sarah-Kelly.title: Sarah Kelly
People/Sarah-Kelly.content:
Sarah Kelly
Biographical Profile

Name: Sarah Kelly
Role in this project: Kelly-line maternal ancestor reconstructed from census extracts and cross-reference mapping.

Source-Cited Facts

Mother of James Kelly (b. 15 Aug 1830).
Cross-reference from ancestors index: KELLEY, Sarah and KELLY, Sarah → UNKNOWN, Sarah (c. 1786 - c. 1867).
1841 Northamptonshire, Peterborough, Cumbergate: Recorded as Sarah KELLY, age 50, female, tailor, born outside county.

Source: HO107, Piece 817, Book 1, ED 1, Folio 10, Page 12; GSU Roll 438883
Household: James Kelly (14, son), George Kelly (6, son), Henry Smith (30, tailor)


1851 Northamptonshire, Peterborough, Cumbergate: Recorded as Sarah KELLEY, head of household, widow, age ~60, almswoman (formerly brought in).

Source: HO107, Piece 1747, Folio 339, Page 46, No: 188; GSU Roll 207423
Birthplace: Cambridgeshire, Whittlesey


1861 Northamptonshire, Peterborough, St John’s Street Almshouse: Recorded as Sarah KELLY, head of household, widow, age ~70, almswoman.

Source: RG9, Piece 966, Folio 155, Page 9; GSU Roll 542729
Birthplace: Cambridgeshire, Whittlesey


Name variants (KELLY/KELLEY) are consistent with census transcription variation; canonical form is Sarah Kelly.

Identity Confirmation
Status: CONFIRMED — Sarah Kelly’s identity across 1841-1861 has been verified as a single individual based on:

Consistent family relationship (mother of James Kelly, b. 1830)
Clear life progression (household head → widow → almshouse resident, 1841-1861)
Consistent birthplace (Cambridgeshire, Whittlesey in later censuses)
Canonical form: Sarah Kelly (primary spelling in majority of entries)
Note: “Barton?” in ancestors index was a filename reference, not a surname clue; no evidence of Barton surname in census records.

Research Gaps

Determine death year (c. 1867) — no 1871 census entry located for Sarah; validate against parish or civil records.
Image-level verification of 1841 and 1851 entries (GSU roll references available; 1861 RG9 piece/folio/page complete).

People/Susan-Lewis.title: Susan Lewis
People/Susan-Lewis.content:
Susan Lewis
Biographical Profile

Name: Susan Lewis
Role in this project: Lewis-line maternal ancestor reconstructed from census extracts and cross-reference mapping.

Source-Cited Facts

Wife of Wynat Lewis; appears in household with Oliver Warren Lewis.
1850 Wisconsin, Racine County, Town of Burlington: Recorded as Susan LEWIS, age 54, female, born Vermont.

Household: Wynat Lewis (68, farmer), Oliver Lewis (25, farmer), and siblings Lousa, Eliza, Martha
Source: Series M432, Roll 1004, Page 150; GSU microfilm available

Household: W.W. Lewis (79, farmer), Oliver Lewis (35), Marshal Lewis (25)
Source: Series M653, Roll 1408, Page 829; GSU microfilm available


Not found in 1880 census; presumed deceased between 1860-1880 (Oliver Lewis’s 1880 household does not include Susan).

Identity Confirmation
Status: CONFIRMED — Susan Lewis’s identity across 1850-1860 verified as single individual based on:

Consecutive census entries (1850, 1860) with consistent family connections
Same household network (Wynat Lewis, Oliver Lewis) across both censuses
Age progression: 54 (1850) → 58 (1860) — variation reflects typical 19th-century census age estimation patterns
Death inference: Not listed in 1880 household; presumed deceased between 1860-1880

Note: Birthplace varies (Vermont 1850, NY 1860) — likely census data quality variation, not identity issue.
Research Gaps

Confirm exact death year and location from Wisconsin vital records or cemetery records.
Image-level verification of both 1850 and 1860 census records (GSU microfilm references available).
Resolve birthplace discrepancy (Vermont vs New York) — may be transcription variation.

People/Uriah-Blake-Lemmon.title: Uriah Blake Lemmon
People/Uriah-Blake-Lemmon.content:
Uriah Blake Lemmon
Biographical Profile

Name: Uriah Blake Lemmon
Role in this project: Lemmon-line ancestor represented in Ohio census-summary household entries from 1850-1880.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 16 Mar 1808; died 15 Feb 1887 (age 78).
Burial: McPherson Cemetery, Clyde, Ohio (inscription: LEMMON); Burial Sites book, page 16.
The Sandusky-Beers biographical sketch for John McIntyre Lemmon identifies Uriah Blake Lemmon and Emily Amanda MacIntyre Lemmon as John McIntyre Lemmon’s parents.

Census Records and Household Context
1850 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township

Head: Euriah B. Lemmon, male, farmer, born New York
Household: Emily A. Lemmon (wife), Wm H. Lemmon (son), John M. Lemmon (son), Sarah A. Lemmon (daughter), Rebecca A. Lemmon (daughter), Cyrus A. Lemmon (son), M. Burton Lemmon (son)
Source: Series M432, Roll 726, Pages 476-477; GSU microfilm available

1860 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township

Head: Uriah B. Lemmon, male, age ~52, master farmer, born New York
Household: Emily A. Lemmon (wife, age ~54), Rebecca A. Lemmon (age ~60, daughter), Cyrus A. Lemmon (son), Emily E. Lemmon (daughter), Nathaniel Lemmon (son)
Source: Series M653, Roll 1032, Page 54; GSU microfilm available

1870 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Green Creek Township, Clyde

Position: Living in household of son John M. Lemmon (lawyer, age ~40)
Head: Jno M. Lemmon, male, lawyer, born Ohio
Household: Uriah B. Lemmon (age ~62, father, retired farmer), John M. Lemmon (son), Annie Lemmon, Mack Lemmon, Annie Havice (housekeeper, Pennsylvania)
Source: Series M593, Roll 1264, Page 125; GSU microfilm available

1880 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Clyde

Head: Uriah B. Lemmon, male, age ~72, retired farmer, born New York
Household: Sarah Lemmon (wife, age ~60, keeping house, born New York)
Source: Series T9, Roll 1063, Page 63A; Fam Hist Lib Film 1255063

Family Connections

Wife: Emily A. Lemmon (first wife, 1850-1860s), then Sarah Lemmon (1880)
Children identified in census: William H., John M., Sarah A., Rebecca A., Cyrus A., M. Burton, Emily E., Nathaniel
Son: John McIntyre Lemmon, lawyer, Civil War veteran, mayor of Clyde, and common pleas judge; his 1870 household included Uriah.
Parents: James Lemmon and Rebecca Blake, based on the Sandusky-Beers sketch’s statement that James and Rebecca were John McIntyre Lemmon’s grandparents.

Family Diagram

graph TD
  UBL["Uriah Blake Lemmon<br/>1808-1887<br/>Master Farmer, Ohio"]
  EA["Emily A. Lemmon<br/>First wife"]
  SL["Sarah Lemmon<br/>Second wife, 1880"]
  
  WH["William H."]
  JM["John M.<br/>Lawyer"]
  SA["Sarah A.<br/>→ Thorpe line"]
  RA["Rebecca A."]
  CA["Cyrus A."]
  MB["M. Burton"]
  EE["Emily E."]
  NL["Nathaniel"]
  
  UBL --> EA
  UBL --> SL
  UBL --> WH
  UBL --> JM
  UBL --> SA
  UBL --> RA
  UBL --> CA
  UBL --> MB
  UBL --> EE
  UBL --> NL

Uriah Blake Lemmon was patriarch of the Ohio Lemmon line, with 8+ documented children across 1850-1880 censuses.
Identity Note
Separate from Uriah Blake Thorpe (1878-1959, Iowa). These are different individuals:

Birth year difference: 70 years (1808 vs 1878)
Different locations: Ohio vs Iowa
Death dates: 1887 vs 1959
Different burial sites: McPherson Cemetery, Clyde vs Evergreen Cemetery, Grand Mound
Likely related through Blake family or Lemmon/Thorpe connection based on pedigree timeline grouping, but definitely different generations.

Research Gaps

Resolve Lemmon/Lemon spelling differences across decades.
Validate apparent OCR ambiguity for sex markers in 1860 entries.
Confirm the 1880 spouse identity against other Lemmon profile records.
Resolve whether the death date is 15 Feb 1887 or 16 Feb 1887; the Sandusky-Beers sketch gives 16 Feb, while earlier vault sources give 15 Feb.
Clarify relationship to Uriah Blake Thorpe via pedigree timeline or family records (possible uncle/nephew or cousin).

Watson Moses Risden
Biographical Profile

Name: Watson Moses Risden
Role in this project: Risden-line household head represented in 1860-1930 census-summary chains.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Watson Moses Risden as born 1 Dec 1843 and died 30 Jun 1932.
The profile includes 1860 Lyons Township, Iowa; 1870 Lapeer, Michigan; 1880 Cedar Rapids, Iowa; 1900 Cedar Rapids, Iowa; 1920 Benton County, Iowa; and 1930 Cedar Rapids, Iowa entries.
The extracted chain places Watson with spouse Nancy and later as father in Anderson household entries.
The Burial Sites book places Watson Moses Risden at Linwood Cemetery in Cedar Rapids, Iowa (page 29), Block 3, 3rd Addition, S½ Lot 135 - Burial 6441, with GPS coordinates 41°57’36.3”N 91°40’25.1”W and date of death 30 June 1932. Map: Google Maps.
The Grand Army records state that Watson Moses Risden was born in Watertown, New York, on 1 Dec 1843; moved westward to Indiana in youth; enlisted in spring 1864 in the 128th Indiana Volunteer Infantry; served at Dalton, Rocky Face, Buzzard’s Roost, Franklin, Nashville, and Kingston; and was mustered out in April 1866.
The same Grand Army biographical page says he came directly to Iowa after the war, lived in Cedar Rapids for more than fifty-five years, was active in G.A.R. Post No. 235 at Cedar Rapids, was elected Iowa Department Commander in 1930, and served as Department Chaplain in 1931 and 1932.

Date Discrepancy

Burial and census-summary material currently give Watson Moses Risden’s death as 30 June 1932.
The Grand Army biographical page says he died on 23 June 1932, shortly after reelection as Department Chaplain.
Keep the canonical date unresolved until checked against cemetery, obituary, probate, or state death records.

Research Gaps

Validate 1900 KING household linkage where Watson and Nancy appear as in-laws.
Reconcile spelling and OCR errors in names/occupations across decades.
Resolve the 23 June 1932 vs 30 June 1932 death-date conflict with independent records.

Sources

People/William-Henry-Palmer.title: William Henry Palmer
People/William-Henry-Palmer.content:
William Henry Palmer
Biographical Profile

Name: William Henry Palmer
Role in this project: Palmer-line patriarch bridging Wisconsin (childhood), Minnesota (farming), and Iowa (final decades); son of John K Palmer; father of May Aleen Palmer.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 12 Jun 1851 (inscribed on headstone); died 8 May 1927, age 75, in Waterloo, Iowa.
Cause of Death: Acute bronchial pneumonia (death-index entry).
Burial: Fairview Cemetery, Waterloo, Iowa; section M, Row 3, Grave 67; inscription WILLIAM H. / JUNE 12, 1851 / MAY 8, 1927 / FATHER.

Census Records and Life Progression
1860 Wisconsin Census — Sauk County, Baraboo (as child)

Head: J PALMER (John K Palmer), male, age 38, farmer
William H. Palmer (son), male, age 9, born Pennsylvania
Source: Series M653, Roll 1429, Page 450; GSU microfilm available

1900 Minnesota Census — Mower County, Frankford Township

Head: William PALMER, male, race White, birthdate Jan 1851, age 49, born Pennsylvania, occupation farmer
Wife: Elizabeth PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Sep 1857, age 42, born Wisconsin, occupation none
Children:

Vern PALMER, male, race White, birthdate Oct 1882, age 18, born Wisconsin, occupation farm laborer
Louis PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Oct 1884, age 16, born Wisconsin
May PALMER, female, race White, birthdate May 1887, age 13, born Wisconsin (later May Aleen Palmer)
Dott PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Aug 1889, age 11, born Wisconsin
Ivas PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Sep 1891, age 8, born Wisconsin
Voila PALMER, female, race White, birthdate May 1896, age 4, born Wisconsin
Vivian PALMER, female, race White, birthdate MAY 1898, age 2, born Minnesota


Source: Series T623, Roll 777, Page 3A; GSU microfilm available

1920 Iowa Census — Blackhawk County, Waterloo, Summer Street

Head: Viola McMillLEN, female, age 28, widow, seamstress/garment factory worker
Household members:

Bernice McMillLEN, female, age 25 (stepdaughter)
William PALMER, male, age 68, father, no occupation
Elizabeth PALMER, female, age 65, mother, no occupation
Maxine WALTON, female, age 6 (stepdaughter)


Note: William and Elizabeth Palmer living with daughter Viola’s household in Iowa by 1920
Source: Series T625, Roll 478, Pages 24B, ED 33; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Father: John K Palmer (1821-1906), Wisconsin farmer
Wife: Elizabeth Palmer (b. Sep 1857 Wisconsin)
Children identified: Vern (b. ~1882), Louis (b. ~1884), May/May Aleen (b. 1887, married Oliver Prior), Dott (b. ~1889), Ivas (b. ~1891), Voila (b. ~1896), Vivian (b. ~1898)
Daughter Viola married, and William and Elizabeth moved to Waterloo to be near family in 1910s-1920s
Pedigree significance: Bridges Wisconsin Palmer (father’s line) to Minnesota farming expansion and Iowa family consolidation

Family Diagram

graph TD
  JKP["John K. Palmer<br/>1821-1906<br/>Wisconsin farmer"]
  WHP["William Henry Palmer<br/>1851-1927<br/>Farmer → Family Elder"]
  EP["Elizabeth Palmer<br/>1857-?<br/>Wisconsin"]
  
  V["Vern"]
  L["Louis"]
  M["May Aleen<br/>→ Oliver Prior"]
  D["Dott"]
  I["Ivas"]
  VO["Voila"]
  VI["Vivian"]
  
  JKP --> WHP
  WHP --- EP
  WHP --> V
  WHP --> L
  WHP --> M
  WHP --> D
  WHP --> I
  WHP --> VO
  WHP --> VI

William Henry Palmer’s trajectory follows the family’s movement from Wisconsin (childhood) to Minnesota (farming years, 1900) to Iowa (family consolidation, 1920).
Research Gaps

Locate William H. Palmer in 1880 census records to trace progression between 1860 Wisconsin and 1900 Minnesota.
Confirm Elizabeth Palmer’s maiden surname and full parentage.
Trace all children’s lives in later records, especially the sons (Vern) and daughters.
Identify Viola’s husband and clarify family consolidation in Iowa.

Sources

People/William-McIntyre.title: William McIntyre
People/William-McIntyre.content:
William McIntyre
Biographical Profile

Name: William McIntyre
Role in this project: Compiled McIntyre-line ancestor and documented father of Major McIntyre in the MacIntyre book outprint.

Source-Cited Facts

The MacIntyre book outprint identifies William McIntyre as probably a son of Thomas and Sarah McIntyre.
The same source gives William McIntyre as born 1 March 1754.
The MacIntyre outprint says William McIntyre married Rosanna Campbell at Chester, Massachusetts, on 25 July 1778, and states that Rosanna was born 14 January 1756.
The source places William McIntyre in the 1790 census at Plantation 7, Hampshire County, Massachusetts.
The source lists William and Rosanna Campbell McIntyre’s children as Betsey, Thomas, William, Zimri, Anna, Major, and Ananias.
The same child list gives Major McIntyre as born 3 May 1789.
The MacIntyre outprint says William McIntyre served in Captain Nathan Rowlee’s company at Ticonderoga from 25 December 1776 to 3 April 1777; enlisted 9 July 1777; served 39 days on a march to reinforce the Northern Army; served seven days in August 1777 with Captain Enoch Shepard’s company on a Bennington alarm; and served one month from 21 September 1777 on a Saratoga expedition.

Family Connections

Possible parents: Thomas McIntyre and Sarah McIntyre, based on the compiled MacIntyre source’s “probably son” wording.
Spouse: Rosanna Campbell.
Child: Major McIntyre.

Research Gaps

Verify William McIntyre’s Revolutionary War service in Massachusetts military records.
Confirm the William-to-Major parent-child link in Chester, Massachusetts, town, church, probate, or land records.
Determine whether the compiled “probably son” link from Thomas and Sarah McIntyre to William is supported by primary evidence.

Sources

Book Outprints — MacIntyre

Source-Cited Facts

The Descendants of Thomas Durfee volume II page 571 says William Tallman was the fourth child of Benjamin Tallman and Patience Durfee Tallman.
Page 571 says William was born at Portsmouth, Rhode Island, on 25 March 1720.
Page 571 says he died on his farm near Harrisonburg, Virginia, on 13 February 1791.
Page 571 says he married Ann Lincoln in Berks County, Pennsylvania, on 20 October 1740 or 1742.
Page 572 says William’s Bible record was copied into the Durfee genealogy but had damaged margins, so some dates were verified or supplemented from other sources.

Family Connections

Spouse: Ann Lincoln Tallman
Child in this project: Benjamin Tallman 1745-1820

People/Wynat-Lewis.title: Wynat Lewis
People/Wynat-Lewis.content:
Wynat Lewis
Biographical Profile

Name: Wynant Williamson Lewis (canonical form)
Name variants: Wynat (census transcription), W.W. (1860 census abbreviation)
Life span: c. 1781 – after 1860 (pedigree timeline estimate)
Role in this project: Early Lewis-line household head and patriarch of Wisconsin branch (1850-1860).

Source-Cited Facts

1850 Wisconsin, Racine County, Town of Burlington: Wynat LEWIS, age 68, male, farmer, born Vermont.

Source: Series M432, Roll 1004, Page 150
Household: Wife Susan (54), children Lousa (37), Eliza (37), Alonzo (33), Oliver (25), Martha (19), Marshall (17), and visitors Erastus Darling (15) and Sarah Darling (12)


1860 Wisconsin, Fond du Lac, Ripon 1st Ward: W.W. LEWIS, age 79, male, farmer, property valued $50.

Source: Series M653, Roll 1408, Page 829
Household: Wife Susan (58), son Oliver (35), son Marshall (25), daughter Elizabeth (24), daughter Martha E. (infant)


Pedigree timeline: Wynant Williamson Lewis c1781-after 1860 — provides full canonical name.
Name evidence: Census age progression (68 → 79 for 10 years) and household consistency confirm same person; W.W. initials match “Wynant Williamson” from pedigree timeline; “Wynat” is transcription variant of “Wynant”.

Household Diagram

graph TD
  WL[Wynat / Wynant Lewis]
  S[Susan]
  L[Louisa]
  E[Eliza]
  A[Alonzo]
  O[Oliver]
  M[Martha / Martha E.]
  MD[Marshall / Marsh]
  ED[Erastus Darling]
  SD[Sarah Darling]

  WL --- S
  WL --> L
  WL --> E
  WL --> A
  WL --> O
  WL --> M
  WL --> MD
  WL --> ED
  WL --> SD

This diagram is a household sketch from the 1850 and 1860 census-summary extracts. It is deliberately literal and should not be read as a completed descendant chart.
Identity Confirmation
Status: CONFIRMED (Spelling Resolved) — Wynant Williamson Lewis’s identity across 1850-1860 verified with canonical form established:

Same household (wife Susan, children Alonzo, Oliver, Martha) across both census years
Age progression consistent: 68 (1850) → 79 (1860) matches 10-year calendar span + aging pattern
Initials W.W. in 1860 confirmed as Wynant Williamson via pedigree timeline
Spelling variants (Wynat/Wynant) are transcription differences, not identity changes
Canonical form: Wynant Williamson Lewis (from pedigree timeline)

Research Gaps

Image-level verification of 1850 and 1860 census pages (M432 and M653 rolls available).
Confirm relationships and household composition in the large 1850 multi-member household.
Verify death date from Wisconsin vital records or cemetery records (timeline says “after 1860”; likely 1860s).

Sources

References/Book-Outprints-Collection.content:
Book Outprints Collection
Overview
A collection of 31 genealogical and historical books with extracted pages (77 total files, primarily GIF images with some PDFs). Sourced from Fairfield County, Ohio historical records and genealogical publications.
Location: /mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/

Spicer Lineage

BOOK 00014 — Descendants of Peter Spicer 1

Thorpe & Miller Lines

BOOK 00008 — Autobiography of Clyde Thorpe

Complete Book List

Known Supporting Materials

Grand Army Records — 2 PDFs on Watson Moses Risden (GAR membership), now indexed in Grand Army Records — Watson Moses Risden
Sandusky-Beers — Lemmon John M entry (pages 607-610), now indexed in Sandusky-Beers — John McIntyre Lemmon
MacIntyre — pages 161-165, now indexed in Book Outprints — MacIntyre

Pilot Extraction Status

Processed the two Watson Moses Risden Grand Army PDFs with pdftotext; extracted text is staged under References/raw/processed/2026-04-24-book-outprints/Risden-Watson-Moses/.
Reviewed Sandusky-Beers/LemmonJohnM607-610.pdf manually from generated page images because pdftotext produced no usable text.
Manually reviewed selected Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller GIF page images (pages 11-15, 240-242, and 250) because local OCR tools were unavailable.
Manually reviewed selected Pioneer People of Fairfield County, Ohio GIF page images (pages 281-283 and 315-317), now indexed in Pioneer People of Fairfield County Ohio.
Manually reviewed MacIntyre GIF page images (pages 161-165), now indexed in Book Outprints — MacIntyre.
Manually reviewed the current Settlers by the Long Grey Trail GIF set (23 page images plus frontispiece), now indexed in Settlers by the Long Grey Trail and the processed extraction index.
Manually reviewed the Tallman-relevant Durfee genealogy pages, now indexed in Durfee and the processed extraction index.
Manually reviewed the Western College page image, now indexed in History of Western College and the processed extraction index.

Next Steps

Install or run OCR for the remaining GIF image books, beginning with Settlers by the Long Grey Trail and other direct-line book folders that currently contain only placeholders.
Continue manual image checks for high-value pages when OCR is unavailable.
Link newly extracted book evidence to relevant people pages with explicit page-level provenance.
Create reconciliation entries for individuals appearing in multiple books.

File Format Notes

Most pages are GIF images (scanned book pages)
77 total files representing 31 books
Some books have multiple pages; others are single-page extracts
PDF files include Grand Army records and Sandusky-Beers Lemmon entry

References/Book-Outprints-—-Durfee.title: Book Outprints — Durfee
References/Book-Outprints-—-Durfee.content:
Durfee
Publication Details

BOOK Number: 030
Family/Subject: Durfee
Source Collection: Book Outprints Collection
Location: Fairfield County, Ohio archives

Description
Durfee family genealogy (11 extracted pages, 2 volumes)
Related People

Benjamin Tallman 1684-1769
Patience Durfee Tallman
William Tallman 1720-1791
Ann Lincoln Tallman
Benjamin Tallman 1745-1820
Dinah Boone

Scope & Content
This book outprint is part of the 2026-04-24 intake batch and is available as extracted page images in the shared intake folder. The Tallman-relevant pages were manually reviewed on 2026-04-29 because local OCR tools were unavailable.
Extracted Evidence from Reviewed Pages

Volume I page 31 says Patience Durfee was a daughter of Thomas Durfee and Deliverance Hall/Tripp Durfee, died at Portsmouth, Rhode Island, in 1723, and married Benjamin Tallman on 23 September 1708.
Volume I page 31 says Benjamin Tallman was a son of Peter Tallman and Joan Briggs Tallman, was born in Portsmouth, Rhode Island, on 28 January 1684, and died at Warwick, Rhode Island, on 20 May 1769.
Volume II page 571 says William Tallman was the fourth child of Benjamin and Patience Durfee Tallman, born at Portsmouth, Rhode Island, on 25 March 1720, and died near Harrisonburg, Virginia, on 13 February 1791.
Volume II page 571 says William Tallman married Ann Lincoln in Berks County, Pennsylvania, on 20 October 1740 or 1742.
Volume II page 571 identifies Ann Lincoln as a daughter of Mordecai Lincoln, gives her probable birth in Chester County, Pennsylvania, on 8 March 1725, says she died near Harrisonburg, Virginia, on 22 December about 1812, and says she was buried beside William.
Volume II page 572 says Benjamin Tallman was the only child of William and Ann Tallman who survived his parents and married.
Volume II page 572 says Benjamin Tallman was born in Berks County, Pennsylvania, on 9 January 1745, died near Canal Winchester, Ohio, on 4 June 1820, married Dinah Boone in Berks County on 9 November 1764, removed to Virginia in 1779, served in Pennsylvania militia and later in Virginia, and removed to Ohio around 1810.
Volume II page 572 says Dinah Boone was born in Berks County, Pennsylvania, on 3 or 10 May 1749, baptized at Morlatton Church on 6 August 1753, died near Canal Winchester, Ohio, on 25 July 1824, and was a first cousin of Daniel Boone.

Research Value

Extends the older Tallman branch behind Benjamin Tallman 1745-1820 through William Tallman and Ann Lincoln Tallman.
Connects the Tallman branch to the earlier Rhode Island Tallman/Durfee generation through Benjamin Tallman 1684-1769 and Patience Durfee Tallman.
Adds migration context: Rhode Island to Berks County, Pennsylvania, to Rockingham County, Virginia, to Canal Winchester, Ohio.
Provides specific follow-up targets: William Tallman’s Bible record, Pennsylvania militia records, Virginia bounty-land records, and Morlatton Church baptism records.

Next Steps

Locate the William Tallman family Bible record referenced on volume II page 572.
Verify Benjamin Tallman’s Pennsylvania and Virginia military-service references.
Verify Dinah Boone’s Morlatton Church baptism.
Check the cited Boone-family manuscripts before relying on the Daniel Boone cousin statement as proven.

Sources

Original book: Fairfield County Book Outprints Collection
Extracted pages: /mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/BOOK 00030 Durfee/
Processed index: Durfee Tallman Extraction Index

References/Book-Outprints-—-Genealogy-an.title: Book Outprints — Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller
References/Book-Outprints-—-Genealogy-an.content:
Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller
Publication Details

BOOK Number: 003
Family/Subject: Miller
Source Collection: Book Outprints Collection
Location: Fairfield County, Ohio archives

Description
Combined genealogy and historical narrative of the Miller family (9 extracted pages)
Related People

Samuel Miller
John Miller
Mathias Miller
Romancy Miller
Miller Mathias Tallman
Lenore Hetty Tallman

Scope & Content
This book outprint is part of the 2026-04-24 intake batch and is available as extracted page images in the shared intake folder. Pages 11-15, 240-242, and 250 were manually reviewed on 2026-04-28 because local OCR tools were unavailable.
Extracted Evidence from Reviewed Pages

Page 11 says Samuel Miller was probably born in York County, Pennsylvania, about 1738, but that no definite birth record had been found.
Page 11 says Samuel Miller married Magdalena Wiley about 1758 or 1759; the author says no record was found for Samuel’s birth or marriage and presents this as a deduction.
Pages 11-12 say Samuel Miller was in York County tax records in 1777 and purchased land in Rockingham County, Virginia, in 1782, about two miles west of Harrisonburg.
Page 12 says a 1785 transfer from Nehemiah Harrison to Samuel Miller was found and that Samuel’s land holdings included 215 acres at purchase and more acreage by the time of his death.
Page 13 says Samuel Miller probably died in 1789 and lists known children: David, Elizabeth, Christly, Michael, Abraham, Barbara, John, Anna, Samuel, and Magdalena.
Pages 14-15 summarize the children of Samuel Miller, including the statement that John Miller entered land west of the Eversole schoolhouse between Thurston and Millersport, Ohio.
Page 15 identifies John Miller as born in York County, Pennsylvania; moving with his parents to Rockingham County, Virginia, about 1782; marrying Anna Smootz; and moving to Fairfield County, Ohio, in 1805.
Page 15 says John Miller settled about two miles south of Millersport, Ohio, on the farm now owned by Lewis Hite.
Page 240 identifies Mathias Miller as born in Rockingham County, Virginia; moved with his parents to Fairfield County, Ohio, about 1805; married Amy E. Critton about 1818; owned land around Millersport; was a founder of the town; and was a contractor in digging the Ohio Canal.
Page 240 gives Mathias Miller as born 7 Jan 1789 and died 4 May 1871.
Pages 240-241 identify Romancy Miller as a child of Mathias Miller and Amy E. Critton, married to Benjamin F. Tallman on 25 Oct 1834 at Millersport, Ohio, with moves to Canal Winchester about 1845, Jones County, Iowa, in 1848, and later Rippey, Greene County, Iowa.
Pages 241-242 identify Miller Mathias Tallman as a child of Romancy Miller and Benjamin Tallman, born 14 Apr 1841.
Page 242 says Mathias M. Tallman married Lizzie Wimmer on 24 Aug 1872 and lists children William Gilmore Tallman and Elvin Lenora Tallman.
Page 242 also has a lower summary headed “Children of Mathias M. Tallman” that gives the children as Willis G. Tallman and Lenore Tallman, matching the names used in census-derived vault summaries more closely than the preceding William Gilmore/Elvin Lenora wording.
Page 250 says Wm. Gilmore Tallman married Edith A. Dean on 10 June 1897 and had Richard D. Tallman and Elvin Lenora Tallman.
Page 250 says Elvin Lenora Tallman married U. B. Thorpe on 22 October 1903, gives U. B. Thorpe as born 14 July 1878, and lists a child Ralph Thorpe born 27 October 1906.

Research Value

Provides compiled-genealogy support for the Samuel Miller to John Miller to Mathias Miller chain.
Connects the Miller line into the Tallman and Thorpe lines through Romancy Miller, Miller Mathias Tallman, and Lenore Hetty Tallman.
Helps reconcile the Tallman child-name variants: William Gilmore/Willis G. and Elvin Lenora/Lenore/Lena.
Adds independent compiled-book support for the Lenore Hetty Tallman and Uriah Blake Thorpe marriage.

Next Steps

Verify Samuel Miller’s York County tax and Rockingham County land records.
Verify John Miller’s land and burial context in Fairfield County, Ohio.
Reconcile the Benjamin Tallman middle initial (B. vs F.).
Reconcile the Miller Mathias Tallman spouse-name conflict (Elizabeth Plomey Ault vs Lizzie Wimmer); the identical October 1841 birth context suggests they may refer to the same person, but primary marriage or death records are still needed.
Determine whether William Gilmore Tallman and Willis G. Tallman are the same person.
Determine whether Elvin Lenora Tallman and Lenore Hetty Tallman are the same person.

Sources

Original book: Fairfield County Book Outprints Collection
Extracted pages: /mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/BOOK 00003 Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller/

References/Book-Outprints-—-Grand-Army-R.content:
Grand Army Records — Watson Moses Risden
Source

Shared Intake files:

/mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/Risden Watson Moses/Grand Army.pdf
/mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/Risden Watson Moses/Grand Army Risden.pdf


Processed text:

References/raw/processed/2026-04-24-book-outprints/Risden-Watson-Moses/Grand-Army.txt
References/raw/processed/2026-04-24-book-outprints/Risden-Watson-Moses/Grand-Army-Risden.txt



Extracted Evidence

The Iowa Grand Army of the Republic overview lists Watson M. Risden among Iowa Department commanders for the June 1928 to June 1933 span.
The biographical page in Grand-Army-Risden.txt states that Watson Moses Risden was born in Watertown, New York, on 1 December 1843 and moved westward to Indiana in youth.
The same page states that he enlisted in spring 1864 in the 128th Indiana Volunteer Infantry, served at Dalton, Rocky Face, Buzzard’s Roost, Franklin, Nashville, and Kingston, and was mustered out in April 1866.
After the war, the page says he moved directly to Iowa and lived in Cedar Rapids for more than fifty-five years.
The page identifies him as active in local Post No. 235 at Cedar Rapids and in the Iowa Department of the Grand Army of the Republic.
It states he was elected Iowa Department Commander at the 1930 encampment, then elected Department Chaplain in 1931 and 1932, and died on 23 June 1932 shortly after reelection.

Research Value
This source materially strengthens the military and civic-service context for Watson Moses Risden. It also introduces a death-date discrepancy: the Grand Army page gives 23 June 1932, while the burial and census-summary material currently used in the vault gives 30 June 1932.
Uncertainties

The Grand Army source is a web-capture/compiled memorial page, not a death certificate.
The date conflict should be resolved against cemetery, obituary, probate, or state death records before changing the canonical death date.

Extracted Evidence from Reviewed Pages

Page 64 names Benjamin Tallman as farm agent during most of the period the college farm was operated by the college, for a time resident agent of the college, and a member of the executive committee.
The page groups Benjamin Tallman with other early supporters of the college and does not provide a middle initial or family relationship.

Look for additional Tallman mentions in the same institutional history if needed.

References/Book-Outprints-—-MacIntyre.title: Book Outprints — MacIntyre
References/Book-Outprints-—-MacIntyre.content:
MacIntyre
Publication Details

BOOK Number: 013
Family/Subject: MacIntyre
Source Collection: Book Outprints Collection
Location: Fairfield County, Ohio archives

Description
MacIntyre family genealogy (5 extracted pages)
Related People

John McIntyre of Kilmoore
William McIntyre
Rosanna Campbell
Major McIntyre
Emily Amanda MacIntyre
Emily McIntyre

Scope & Content
This book outprint is part of the 2026-04-24 intake batch and is available as extracted page images in the shared intake folder. Pages 161-165 were manually reviewed on 2026-04-28 because local OCR tools were unavailable.
Extracted Evidence from Reviewed Pages

Page 161 identifies John McIntyre of Kilmoore as born in the parish of Kilmoore, County Tyrone, Ireland, about 1678; as having come from Ireland before 1719; as having died at Rutland, Massachusetts, on 26 May 1759; and as married to Grace, who survived him.
Pages 161-162 list children of John and Grace McIntyre, including Agnes, Janet, Elizabeth, Martha, Mary, Christian, John, James, and Thomas.
Page 163 says the younger John McIntyre, child of John and Grace, served in Massachusetts companies during the American Revolution, including service connected with the 19 April 1775 alarm, the 1776 march to Mount Independence, the 1777 Bennington alarm, and a 1782 Northampton alarm.
Pages 163-164 identify William McIntyre as probably a son of Thomas and Sarah McIntyre, born 1 March 1754, married at Chester, Massachusetts, on 25 July 1778 to Rosanna Campbell, and recorded in the 1790 census at Plantation 7, Hampshire County, Massachusetts.
Page 164 says William McIntyre served in Captain Nathan Rowlee’s company at Ticonderoga from 25 December 1776 to 3 April 1777, enlisted 9 July 1777, served 39 days on a march to reinforce the Northern Army, served seven days in August 1777 with Captain Enoch Shepard’s company on a Bennington alarm, and served one month from 21 September 1777 on a Saratoga expedition.
Page 164 lists William and Rosanna Campbell McIntyre’s children as Betsey, Thomas, William, Zimri, Anna, Major, and Ananias, with Major McIntyre born 3 May 1789.
Page 165 identifies Alexander McIntyre, son of Alexander and Elizabeth Robinson McIntyre, as a physician and surgeon in Palmyra, New York, and lists his son Samuel Beckwith McIntyre as a Palmyra lawyer, Civil War officer, and GAR member.
Page 165 also records an unrelated or not-yet-connected James McIntire, born in Ireland about 1838, who enlisted in the 12th New Hampshire Regiment during the Civil War and was wounded in 1864.

Research Value

Provides compiled-genealogy support for the William McIntyre to Major McIntyre parent-child link.
Adds Revolutionary War service context for William McIntyre and an older John McIntyre.
Extends the compiled McIntyre line back to County Tyrone, Ireland, through John McIntyre of Kilmoore, but this older chain remains dependent on the compiled source until primary records are checked.

Next Steps

Verify William McIntyre’s Revolutionary War service in Massachusetts military records.
Verify William and Rosanna Campbell McIntyre’s children in Chester, Massachusetts, town or church records.
Determine whether the compiled line from John McIntyre of Kilmoore through Thomas to William can be supported by primary records.
Confirm the exact relationship between Major McIntyre, Sarah Shutts, and Emily Amanda MacIntyre from non-burial records.

Sources

Original book: Fairfield County Book Outprints Collection
Extracted pages: /mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/BOOK 00013 MacIntyre/

References/Book-Outprints-—-Pioneer-Peop.filePath: References/Book Outprints — Pioneer People of Fairfield County Ohio.md
References/Book-Outprints-—-Pioneer-Peop.title: Book Outprints — Pioneer People of Fairfield County Ohio
References/Book-Outprints-—-Pioneer-Peop.content:
Pioneer People of Fairfield County Ohio
Source

Publication: Pioneer Period and Pioneer People of Fairfield County, Ohio
Shared Intake folder: /mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/BOOK 00029 Pioneer People of Fairfield County Ohio/
Pages reviewed: title page and pages 281-283, 315-317
Extraction note: Local OCR tools were unavailable, so these pages were manually reviewed from GIF page images.

Extracted Evidence

Pages 281-283 contain a Tallman sketch for Sarah Wells Tallman and Samuel Tallman.
Page 281 states that Sarah Wells married Samuel Tallman at Wellsburg, Virginia, on 28 March 1801, and that the couple moved from Wellsburg to Fairfield County, Ohio, in 1804.
Page 282 describes Samuel Tallman as a carpenter and cabinetmaker who built many log houses in Greenfield Township and died at the early age of 50.
Page 282 says Samuel Tallman’s father and grandfather, both named William Tallman, moved from Pennsylvania/Rhode Island to Virginia during the American Revolution; it also states that Samuel’s mother, Dinah Boone, was a cousin of Daniel Boone.
Page 282 states that Benjamin Tallman and Dinah Boone settled at Canal Winchester, Ohio.
Page 283 names children of Samuel and Sarah Wells Tallman: William, Susannah Harrison, Amrah Scothern, and Phebe Hooker. It also describes Sarah’s difficult trip to Ohio with her brother-in-law William Tallman.
Pages 315-317 discuss the Hooker/Tallman connection. Page 316 states that Richard Hooker married Nancy Tallman on 6 January 1806, names Nancy as a daughter of William Tallman, and says William donated land for the East Ringgold cemetery.

Research Value
This source gives narrative context for the older Tallman migration into Fairfield County, Ohio. It does not by itself prove the entire Tallman chain down to Miller Mathias Tallman, but it helps explain the Ohio settlement context behind the Tallman branch already documented by census, burial, pedigree, and Miller genealogy sources.
Uncertainties

The older Tallman relationships are compiled narrative evidence and need primary-source checks in land, probate, cemetery, or church records.
The source links Samuel Tallman to Benjamin and Dinah Boone Tallman by narrative context, but the exact relationship to John Tallman and Benjamin B Tallman still requires reconciliation against the pedigree timeline and civil records.

References/Book-Outprints-—-Sandusky-Bee.filePath: References/Book Outprints — Sandusky-Beers Lemmon John M.md
References/Book-Outprints-—-Sandusky-Bee.title: Book Outprints — Sandusky-Beers Lemmon John M
References/Book-Outprints-—-Sandusky-Bee.content:
Sandusky-Beers — John McIntyre Lemmon
Source

Publication: Commemorative Biographical Record of the Counties of Sandusky and Ottawa, Ohio
Publisher: J. H. Beers & Co., Chicago
Year: 1896
Pages reviewed: title page and pages 607-610
Shared Intake file: /mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/Sandusky-Beers/LemmonJohnM607-610.pdf
Extraction note: pdftotext returned no usable text because this PDF is image-only. Claims below were manually checked against temporary page images generated from the PDF.

Extracted Evidence

Page 607 begins a biographical sketch for John McIntyre Lemmon and describes him as a citizen, soldier, bar member, and judge.
Page 607 states that John McIntyre Lemmon was born 25 July 1839 in Townsend, Sandusky County, Ohio, son of Uriah Blake Lemmon and Emily Amanda MacIntyre Lemmon.
Pages 607-608 trace the Lemmon paternal line to Hugh Lemmon, then to James Lemmon Sr., who was born in Northumberland County, Pennsylvania, on 17 July 1779; married Rebecca Blake in 1805; volunteered in the War of 1812; moved to Ohio in 1827; and died at North Ridge, Townsend, Sandusky County, Ohio, on 7 May 1854.
Page 608 states that Uriah Blake Lemmon died at Clyde, Ohio, on 16 February 1887, and that his wife died 12 July 1860 in Townsend Township, Sandusky County.
Pages 608-610 summarize John McIntyre Lemmon’s education, law study, Civil War service, promotion, marriage, public office, and death.
Page 610 states that John McIntyre Lemmon married Annie Covell on 29 March 1864 at Perkins, Erie County, Ohio; was elected first mayor of Clyde on 11 June 1866; was appointed judge of the common pleas court in October 1866; toured France, England, Scotland, and Wales in summer 1887; and died at his home in Clyde on 17 August 1895.

Research Value
This source is a compiled county biography, not a vital record. It is nevertheless valuable because it directly identifies the parent-child relationship between John McIntyre Lemmon, Uriah Blake Lemmon, and Emily Amanda MacIntyre, and it clarifies that James Lemmon and Rebecca Blake are John McIntyre Lemmon’s grandparents.
Uncertainties

The PDF needs OCR or full manual transcription for exact quotation-level use.
The page gives 16 February 1887 for Uriah Blake Lemmon’s death, while the existing vault page records 15 February 1887 from earlier compiled sources; keep this as a discrepancy until checked against cemetery, probate, or death records.

References/Book-Outprints-—-Settlers-by-.title: Book Outprints — Settlers by the Long Grey Trail
References/Book-Outprints-—-Settlers-by-.content:
Settlers by the Long Grey Trail
Publication Details

BOOK Number: 004
Family/Subject: Regional
Source Collection: Book Outprints Collection
Location: Fairfield County, Ohio archives

Description
Regional genealogy and history of settlers (26 extracted pages)
Related People

Elizabeth Harrison
John Tallman
Nathaniel Harrison
Mary Woodley

Scope & Content
This book outprint is part of the 2026-04-24 intake batch and is available as extracted page images in the shared intake folder. The current source folder contains 23 page images plus a frontispiece. The pages were manually reviewed on 2026-04-29 because local OCR tools were unavailable.
Extracted Evidence from Reviewed Pages

Page 312 lists Nathaniel Harrison as a child of Capt. Reuben Harrison and first wife Lydia Harrison.
Page 312 says Nathaniel Harrison died in 1807 and married Mary Woodley in 1784.
Pages 374-375 identify Mary Woodley as a daughter of Jacob Woodley and Grace Looker.
Page 375 says Nathaniel Harrison died testate in 1807 and left children named Lydia, Phebe, Gracy, Anna, Elizabeth, Mary, Jerusha, Edith, Lucretia, Samuel, and Nathaniel.
Page 375 says litigation over Nathaniel Harrison’s heirs identified Elizabeth as wife of John Tallman.
Page 375 says a 10 March 1817 deed included John Tallman and wife Elizabeth, of Fairfield County, Ohio, conveying their share of Nathaniel Harrison land.
Page 376 lists Elizabeth among Nathaniel and Mary Harrison’s children, gives her marriage as 1808 to John Tallman, and says she removed to Ohio.

Research Value

Provides compiled-book support for the Elizabeth Harrison and John Tallman marriage.
Adds a Harrison/Woodley parentage trail for Elizabeth Harrison through Nathaniel Harrison and Mary Woodley.
Gives a specific primary-record target: the 1817 deed involving John Tallman and wife Elizabeth, of Fairfield County, Ohio.
Helps explain why Rockingham County, Virginia records matter for the older Fairfield County, Ohio Tallman line.

Next Steps

Locate Nathaniel Harrison’s original 1807 probate or estate records.
Locate the Burkholder litigation or chancery case summarized on page 375.
Locate the 10 March 1817 deed naming John Tallman and wife Elizabeth.
Confirm John Tallman’s exact birth, death, and parentage from primary Tallman-side records.

Sources

Original book: Fairfield County Book Outprints Collection
Extracted pages: /mnt/g/My Drive/Thorpe Shared Intake/Book Outprints/Fairfield County outprint/BOOK 00004 Settlers by the Long Grey Trail/
Processed index: Settlers by the Long Grey Trail Extraction Index

References/Butch-Thorpe-Email.title: Butch Thorpe Email
References/Butch-Thorpe-Email.content:
Butch Thorpe Email
Source file: References/raw/butch-thorpe-email.txt
Sender and Context

Sender signs as Butch (nickname for Robert “Butch” Thorpe).
Message sent to Zach with interpretation guidance for attached pedigree timeline files.

Extracted Statements

Attachments are intended as a resource-finding aid and include pedigrees.
Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Not all ancestors with records are shown; only those from earliest generations that fit on the page.
Red dots denote official record types, with nearby numbers referencing Butch’s personal copies.
Blue dots denote census records at timeline dates.
Butch can provide a PDF describing the record types he has collected.

Notes
This email provides interpretation metadata for the .cdr pedigree files and should be cited when describing how timeline markers are encoded.

Shared Intake 2026-04-24: Census InDesign Summaries
Overview
This intake batch contains 88 individual Census InDesign summary PDFs extracted from the shared intake folder. Each PDF is a professionally formatted single-page census summary for one person, compiled from UK Census records (primarily 1841-1871).
Source Format & Quality

Format: InDesign-compiled PDFs with structured census data tables
Coverage: Primarily UK censuses (HO107, RG9, RG10, RG11, RG12, RG13 series)
Time span: 1841-1871 (some individuals span wider date ranges)
Data included per person:

Birth/death dates (when available)
Multiple census year entries with locations
Household composition (relationships, ages, occupations)
Precise source citations (class, piece, folio, page, GSU roll)
Geographic locations (parish, township, street address)

Extraction Method
Text extracted from PDF using pdftotext -layout to preserve table structure:
pdftotext -layout "CensusSummary-{FirstName}{LastName}.pdf" "CensusSummary-{FirstName}{LastName}.txt"
Files Generated

Individual .txt files for each of 88 people: CensusSummary-{FirstName}{LastName}.txt
Located in: References/raw/inbox/2026-04-24-census-indesign/

Next Steps for Integration

Cross-reference these summaries with existing People Directory entries
For individuals without existing pages, create new profiles with this census data
For individuals with existing pages, augment with detailed census household and location information
Create additional reference pages by thematic group (e.g., UK parishes, occupation clusters)
Mark ambiguous identities (e.g., Eleanor Emblow vs Eleanor Ireland) for reconciliation

Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Citation Notes
Source files:

References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/Ancestors in the Census.txt

Context

Files were staged from shared intake in text-note form.
These notes compile citation references for UK and selected US census appearances tied to Thorpe-line ancestors.

Extracted Statements

The notes include UK citation references for 1841, 1861, 1871, and 1901 census years using Class/Piece/Folio/Page formats (for example HO107, RG9, RG10, RG13).
Individuals listed include Bellamy, Sorrell, Thorogood, Emblow, Kelly, Whitfield, and Munson/Monson variants.
The notes include US citation entries for 1900 with roll/page/enumeration district formats and names including Clyde Thorpe and Joseph Prior.
English Census Notes.txt links individual image filenames to citation metadata, indicating potential image-level validation paths.

Uncertainty Notes

Person-name spelling varies across files, including Monson/Munson and Witfield/Whitfield.
Notes include possible or inferred matches in some filename annotations and should be treated as provisional until image checks are complete.

Processing scope

Parsed and reviewed pages 1-10 of CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf.
This is a partial extraction pass focused on high-confidence entries in early pages.

Extracted statements

The document title page identifies the work as a census summary for individual ancestors of Lancelot and Wendolyn Thorpe, compiled by Robert Thorpe in October 2009.
The introduction states the intended census coverage range is 1841 through 1930 and that the index lists direct ancestors.
The contents pages include direct-ancestor entries for AULT, Andrew, AULT, Elizabeth Plomey, and AULT, Frederick.
Page 9 includes an 1850 Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio household for Andrew Ault (age 82, farmer, born New York) with Elizabeth Ault (age 83, born New York) and Amy Palmer (age 17, born Ohio), cited to Series M432, Roll 699, Page 523.
Page 10 includes an Elizabeth Plomey Ault section with census entries for 1850, 1860, 1880, 1900, 1910, and 1920, including repeated Tallman-household entries for Miller M. Tallman and Elizabeth P. Tallman.
Page 10 entries identify Lena/Lena H. Tallman as daughter in 1880 and 1900 records and include birthplace fields and household relationships.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing may contain minor transcription artifacts in abbreviations and column headers.
Extracted statements are limited to pages 1-10 and do not yet include deeper person-specific pages after page 10.
Compiler contact details visible in the introduction were not propagated to person pages.

Processing scope

This extraction pass covers entries from Ault, Bangle, Baxter/Rowland, Bellamy, Beneworth/Thorogood, and Blake/Lemmon sections.

Extracted statements

Page 11 includes AULT, Frederick with the 1850 Salisbury Township, Meigs County, Ohio household and citation Series M432, Roll 710, Page 175.
Pages 12-13 include BANGLE, Elizabeth Maria and BANGLE, Louis S Jr with recurring 1860 Michigan and 1870/1880 Wisconsin household entries and citations including M653 Roll 1407 and M593 Roll 1712.
Page 14 includes BAXTER, Matilda with 1850 Indiana and 1860/1870 Iowa Rowland household entries and citation Series M432, Roll 154, Page 241.
Pages 15-18 include Bellamy branch entries (Henry James, James, James Archibald, Richard) with repeated 19th/early 20th century Lincolnshire/Peterborough census extracts and multiple UK source references (HO107, RG9, RG10, RG11, RG12, RG13, RG14 tokens as listed).
Page 19 includes BENEWORTH, Hannah with 1841/1861 Thorogood-linked entries in Felsted, Essex.
Page 20 includes BLAKE, Rebecca with an 1850 Townsend Township, Sandusky County, Ohio Lemmon household entry and citation Series M432, Roll 726, Page 476.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing introduces visible spelling and formatting inconsistencies in some names and place strings.
Several UK entries on these pages contain incomplete piece/folio/page fields in the compiled table text and require image-level verification.
Some household role interpretations are compiler-normalized and should be treated as provisional until direct page-image checks are complete.

This extraction pass covers Brooks/Ault crossover, Burdick-Risden, Burgett-Spicer, Bangle/Caswell, Critton-Miller, Emblow/Kelly, Filkins/Johnson-Spicer, and Greenwood mortality entries.

Pages 23-24 (BURGETT, Mary and BURGETT, William) include 1850 Iowa County, Iowa Brush Run Township Bergett household entries; page 23 also includes Spicer household tables for 1860/1870/1880/1900/1910 linked to Mary Burgett in mother/mother-in-law roles.
Page 25 (CASWELL, Angeline) includes repeated Bangle household entries (1860/1870/1880) plus a 1900 Eau Claire, Wisconsin entry for Cazwell BANGLE.
Page 26 (CRITTON, Amy E.) includes 1850/1860/1870 Walnut Township, Fairfield County, Ohio Miller-household entries with Mathias and Amy/Anna Miller.
Pages 27-28 include EMBLOW, Joseph and EMBLOW, Martha with UK census entries from 1841 through 1901 and citations including HO107, RG9, and RG10 references in listed years.
Page 29 (FILKINS, Catherine) includes 1850 New York Nunda Johnson household and 1860 West Sparta entry containing Catherine Johnson.
Page 30 (GREENWOOD, Mary) includes an 1850 Iowa mortality schedule entry for Mary Bergett with death month/cause notes.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing introduces visible spelling and formatting inconsistencies in names and occupations.
Some UK entries in this slice include incomplete piece/folio/page fields and require image-level verification.
Several entries appear compiler-crosslinked across surnames and need identity continuity checks before deeper relationship assertions.

Pages 46-48 include MUNSON, Arthur Jr, MUNSON, Emily, and MUNSON, James, with 1841-1911 Essex census chains and multiple UK record citations (HO107, RG9, RG10, RG11, RG12, RG13, RG14 references as listed).
Page 49 (PALMER, John K) includes 1860, 1880, and 1900 Wisconsin entries.

This extraction pass is Prior/Palmer/Risden-heavy and includes repeated household chains already seen in earlier page ranges, plus Peter Palmer and Quackenbush-indexed entries.

Extracted statements

Pages 53-56 repeat Prior clusters: PRIOR, Arthur Edwin, PRIOR, Joseph Warren, PRIOR, Oliver Warren, PRIOR, Ruby Bernice with household chains in Ohio, Michigan, Minnesota, South Dakota, and Iowa.
Page 57 (QUACKENBUSH, Elizabeth A) links to Lewis household extracts from 1860 Wisconsin and 1880 Minnesota.
Pages 58-60 include Risden entries: RISDEN, Hattie May, RISDEN, John Wheeler, and RISDEN, Watson Moses with repeated 1880-1930 Iowa/Michigan-linked household evidence and citations.

Uncertainty notes

Multiple records in this slice repeat prior page extractions and should be treated as corroborative duplicates unless image-level deltas are found.
OCR introduces substantial variance (for example Voila/Viola, foundary/foundry, KIING/KING, and occasional malformed relationship/sex markers).
Some extracted table fields conflict or truncate and must be validated against original census images before high-confidence relationship assertions.

Context

Files were staged from the shared intake crawl dated 2026-04-22.
These are text extracts and index/transcription notes rather than complete image certificates.

Extracted Statements

CERT0058PalmerWilliaHenry-Death Record.txt states there is no attached certificate image and records a death-index entry for William H. Palmer in Waterloo, Iowa on 8 May 1927, age 75, with cause listed as acute bronchial pneumonia.
JamesKellyBaptism1830.txt gives James Kelly christening date as 15 Aug 1830 at Peterborough, Northampton, England, with parents William Kelly and Sarah.
WilliamKellyBurial1840.txt gives a burial entry for William Kelly on 06 Mar 1840 in Peterborough, age 52.
JohnKirbyAliceMeadowsMarriage1752.txt gives marriage entry date 29 May 1752 in Oundle, Northamptonshire for John Kirby and Alice Meadows.
WilliamEmblowKeziaKirbyMarriage1786.txt gives marriage entry date 13 Jul 1786 in Oundle, Northamptonshire for William Emblow and Kezia Kirby, with witness names John Kirby and Eliz Bellamy.

Uncertainty Notes

Several entries are transcribed/indexed summaries and may not reflect full image-side details.
Name spellings and abbreviations should be verified against original register images when available.

Overview

Source Format & Quality

Format: InDesign-compiled PDFs with structured census data tables
Coverage: Primarily UK censuses (HO107, RG9, RG10, RG11, RG12, RG13 series)
Time span: 1841-1871 (some individuals span wider date ranges)
