Spicer Pedigree Timeline (family lineage)
Book Outprints Collection (burial documentation)

Topics/Spicer-Risden-Branch-Summary.title: Spicer Risden Branch Summary
Topics/Spicer-Risden-Branch-Summary.content:
Spicer and Risden Branch Summary
This branch follows an Iowa-centered Spicer family story from nineteenth-century farming households into twentieth-century industrial work. It is one of the most visitor-ready parts of the vault because several profiles already include repeated census households, burial-site notes, and compact family diagrams.
Branch Diagram

graph TD
  NS["Nathan Spicer<br/>1796-1873<br/>Iowa patriarch"]
  CRS["Charles Russell Spicer<br/>1822-1887<br/>farmer / chair maker"]
  GBS["George B. Spicer<br/>1864-1938<br/>Iowa farmer"]
  HMR["Hattie May Risden<br/>1877-1967<br/>Lemmon/Thorpe connection"]
  LHS["Lester Harold Spicer<br/>1906-1974<br/>industrial worker"]
  RBP["Ruby Bernice Prior<br/>1913-2006"]

  NS -. "lineage lead" .-> CRS
  CRS -. "relationship needs confirmation" .-> GBS
  GBS --- HMR
  GBS --> LHS
  HMR --> LHS
  LHS --- RBP

Dashed arrows mark lineage links that are useful for navigation but still need direct record confirmation.
Start With These People

Nathan Spicer - early Spicer patriarch documented in 1850-1870 Iowa census households and burial-site notes.
Charles Russell Spicer - bridge-generation Spicer profile with census households from 1850-1880.
George B. Spicer - Iowa farmer and household head whose page anchors the later Spicer family.
Hattie May Risden - connects the Spicer story back to the Lemmon/Thorpe branch.
Lester Harold Spicer and Ruby Bernice Prior - later-generation couple linking the Spicer and Prior lines.

What We Know

Nathan Spicer appears across 1850, 1860, and 1870 Iowa census summaries, moving from a blacksmith-related household to dependent residence with family.
George B. Spicer is documented as a child or young adult in 1870 and 1880, then as a household head with Hattie May Risden in 1900-1920.
Lester Harold Spicer is tracked from child in George and Hattie’s farming household to railroad and manufacturing work in Cedar Rapids.
The Spicer/Spooner and Spring Grove cemetery entries provide burial context for several members of this branch.

What Remains Uncertain

The exact Nathan-to-Charles-to-George relationship chain remains a research gap on the person pages and should not be treated as fully proved.
Some census extracts contain OCR or compiler ambiguity, especially around household relationships and name forms.
The Ruby Bernice Prior connection is source-backed by staged lineage, census summaries, the Prior timeline, and burial notes, but her page still flags parentage correlation as an open task.

Sources

Nathan Spicer
Charles Russell Spicer
George B. Spicer
Hattie May Risden
Lester Harold Spicer
Ruby Bernice Prior
Census InDesign Summaries
Burial Sites Summary
Spicer Lineage Note

Butch’s Notes on How to Read the Timelines
Per Butch’s email:

Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Red dots indicate official records.
Numbers by red dots refer to Butch’s personal record copies.
Blue dots indicate census records corresponding to the date marked by vertical lines.
The charts show selected early generations that fit on each page, not all ancestors with records.

Current Limitation
The files are CorelDRAW (.cdr) documents and require export to PDF or image before structured transcription into person pages can proceed.
The repository now also has extracted text for the charts, which is enough to mine branch names, timeline groupings, and discrepancy candidates.

PersonTimeline TextOther Vault EvidenceNotesArthur Edwin Prior1853-1929Census-summary page says 1851-1929Likely needs image-level confirmation before the birth year is treated as settled.John Wheeler Risdenc1816-1884Census-summary and burial pages say 1812-1892This is the strongest date conflict surfaced by the timeline text.Elizabeth A Quackenbushc1841-1909Census-summary page says c1836-1909Could be a different person or an OCR/lineage-chart mismatch.James Kellyc1828-before 1881?Parish and census pages point to christening in 1830 and later family entriesThe chart text may be a different James Kelly or an error in the compiled timeline.
Record-Reference Leads

The extracted text includes identifiers like LVLZ-FHQ, LWX6-WZ9, K2TN-858, KGMR-XXX, and LHK4-7HG.
Treat these as search leads only until they are tied back to a known record system or person-copy index.

Topics/UK-Parish-and-Regional-Context.title: UK Parish and Regional Context
Topics/UK-Parish-and-Regional-Context.content:
UK Parish and Regional Context
Overview
The Thorpe family research vault captures ancestors documented in UK parishes during the 1841–1871 census era, primarily concentrated in the East Midlands and East Anglia regions. This page provides geographic context for understanding settlement patterns and census documentation.
Primary Parishes and Regions
Lincolnshire (East Midlands)
Key Families: Bellamy, Kelly, Emblow, Sorrell, Thorogood

Bourn — Center of Bellamy family documentation (1841–1871)

James Bellamy (1787–c.1867) — Miller, later Bedehouse Man
Richard Bellamy — Agricultural laborer
Henry James Bellamy — Related line


Lincolnshire counties documentation: HO107 (1841), RG9 (1861), RG10 (1871) series
Migration pattern: Rural Lincolnshire to urban Bedhouses in later life (economic transition)

Related Regions (Implied from Census References)
Somerset/Devon — Sorrell and Thorogood surname distribution suggests West Country origin

Agricultural communities in 1841–1871 census era
Family names scattered across parishes but not yet reconciled to specific locations

County Tyrone, Ireland / Massachusetts — McIntyre compiled-genealogy origin trail

William McIntyre (1754–after 1789) documented in the MacIntyre book outprint as father of Major McIntyre
The same outprint traces an older compiled line to John McIntyre of Kilmoore, County Tyrone, Ireland, in Massachusetts before 1719
US settlement continued into Ohio and Wisconsin in later generations

Census Series and Documentation



































Census YearSeriesCoverageKey Families1841HO107English parishesBellamy, Kelly, Sorrell, Thorogood1851HO107English parishesBellamy (Bedhouses transition)1861RG9English parishesBellamy, related lines1871RG10English parishesLimited; many families in US by this time
Research Implications
Known Gaps

Pre-1841 UK sources (parish registers, Poor Law records) not yet incorporated
Specific street addresses and township boundaries in Lincolnshire not yet mapped
Cemetery/burial locations in UK parishes not yet systematized

Next Steps for Parish Research

Locate Bourn parish registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) for Bellamy line 1787–1840
Map Lincolnshire agricultural communities to identify other family branches
Research Poor Law settlement records for urban transitions (Bedhouse entries)
Verify Somerset/Devon connection for Sorrell/Thorogood lines via parish records

index.title: Thorpe Family History
index.content:
Thorpe Family History

This site gathers the Thorpe family history shared by Robert “Butch” Thorpe and turns it into a source-grounded family wiki. It follows the Thorpe line through connected Lemmon, Blake, Spicer, Risden, Palmer, Prior, Lewis, Ault, and Tallman families.
The best way to begin is with one of the branch stories below. Each branch page gives a plain-language overview, a compact family diagram, links to the strongest person profiles, and a short list of what still needs verification.
Start With a Family Branch

























BranchWhat to expectLemmon, Blake, and ThorpeThe core Thorpe-connected branch context from the compiled pedigree timeline, including the two separate Uriah Blake identities and the Sarah Annett Lemmon bridge into the Thorpe line.Spicer and RisdenAn Iowa farming line from Nathan Spicer through George B. Spicer, Hattie May Risden, and Lester Harold Spicer.Palmer, Prior, and LewisA migration story from Wisconsin and Minnesota into Iowa, connecting Wynant Williamson Lewis, Martha Eliza Lewis, May Aleen Palmer, and the Prior family.Ault and TallmanOhio-to-Iowa household records for the Ault and Tallman families, including Elizabeth Plomey Ault, Miller Mathias Tallman, Benjamin B Tallman, and Romancy Miller.
Explore by Need

Find a person: Use the People Directory for the full alphabetical list.
Search by topic or source: Use the Search Index.
See the source trail: Start with Butch Thorpe Email, Thorpe Pedigree Timelines, and Census InDesign Summaries.
Follow recent work: Read the Changelog for completed reconciliations, newly enriched profiles, and publishing notes.

What This Site Is
This is a research wiki, not a finished family book. Some pages are well supported by census summaries, burial records, and pedigree timeline exports; other pages are still placeholders for future verification. When the evidence is uncertain, the page should say so.
The current vault documents 111 people and includes census extracts, burial-site notes, compiled pedigree timelines, and references to 31 genealogical or historical book extracts. The strongest visitor-ready pages are linked from the branch summaries above.
